Public demo 023 · replay · falsification · independent review

Replay the proof.
Try to break
the claim.

A browser-local gauntlet for the core GoalOS standard: a claim does not become stronger because it is polished. It becomes stronger only when the evidence can be replayed, compared, challenged, bounded, and handed to a human reviewer.

Open the gauntlet Claim Firewall

No user data wanted. Use only public-safe demo text. Do not enter personal, confidential, regulated, customer, wallet, seed phrase, private-key, or proprietary information. This page uses No account, No cookies, No analytics, No storage, No wallet, No network request, and No external action.

Replay verdict

Awaiting replay.

Run the gauntlet to see whether the claim survives replay, baseline comparison, validator challenge, privacy boundary, and drift checks.

PENDING
Replayability—
Falsifiability—
Evidence integrity—
Promotion posture—

Baseline ladder

    Replay findings

      Falsification result

        Replay

        If it cannot be replayed, it cannot settle.

        The demo teaches that a proof packet needs trace roots, output hashes, environment pins, baseline context, and replay instructions before it can support a stronger claim.

        Falsification

        A proof system must show what would break it.

        Missing baselines, validator collusion, privacy leakage, silent drift, and hidden costs are not cosmetic issues. They change the verdict.

        Human boundary

        Surviving the gauntlet is not automatic authority.

        The page stops at review-ready status. It emits a docket; it does not certify factual correctness, authorize funds, or activate production authority.

        Chronicle trace

        Replay log

          ReplayReceipt JSON

          Downloadable public-safe docket

          Run the replay gauntlet to generate a ReplayReceipt.
          GoalOS core idea

          The next proof is not another claim.
          It is a replay people can challenge.

          GoalOS turns public demonstrations into proof rooms: claims, baselines, proof packets, replay receipts, validator notes, risk ledgers, rollback boundaries, and review decisions.

          ClaimBaselineTraceReplayFalsifyBoundaryReviewChronicle
